headline: Samsung, SK Hynix Drive AI Chip Rally; Oil Settles Pre-Conflict Levels
summary: Samsung Electronics reported record Q2 operating profit of 89.4 trillion KRW, up 1,810.3% YoY, fueled by the AI semiconductor supercycle. SK Hynix plans a $28 billion US listing to capitalize on the AI wave, while TSMC expects nearly 40% revenue growth. Oil prices settled at pre-Iran war levels, and Fed official Waller noted inflation risks remain high.

bullets.lead: Samsung Electronics posted record Q2 operating profit, surpassing Nvidia.

segments.text: The company reported 89.4 trillion KRW in Q2 operating profit, an 1,810.3% increase year-over-year, driven by the AI semiconductor supercycle.

sources.title: 삼성전자, 영업익 89조 '또 신기록'…엔비디아도 넘었다(상보)

bullets.lead: SK Hynix plans a $28 billion US listing to capitalize on the global AI wave.

segments.text: The offering aims to ride the AI boom, with a hedge fund run by an ex-OpenAI researcher already betting on the IPO.

sources.title: SK Hynix’s Planned $28 Billion Offering Brings Korean Market Fever to the U.S.
South Korea's SK Hynix launches $28 billion US listing to ride global AI wave
Hedge fund run by ex-OpenAI researcher bets on SK Hynix’s US IPO

bullets.lead: TSMC is expected to raise its full-year revenue outlook to nearly 40% growth.

segments.text: This upward revision from "over-30%" is driven by strong orders from key clients like Nvidia, AMD, and Apple amidst the AI megatrend.

bullets.lead: Intel's 18A process yield has reportedly improved, with production ramping up.

segments.text: The company has fixed 18A wafer-to-wafer yield variability, with Oregon and Arizona facilities now producing approximately 30,000 wafers per month.

bullets.lead: Samsung Electronics reported a record 89.4 trillion KRW operating profit for Q2.

segments.text: This figure represents an 1,810.3% increase from the previous year, signaling a strong performance in the semiconductor sector.

x_themes.topic: NVIDIA AI factory architecture complexity and delays
x_themes.summary: NVIDIA's full-stack AI factory architecture, including optics, power, and thermals, is becoming more complex, potentially widening its moat but also causing tactical delays and raising concerns about customer pushback.

x_themes.topic: Foxconn's strong AI server and CPO switch demand
x_themes.summary: Foxconn reported soaring Q2 revenue driven by strong global demand for AI servers and networking products, with expectations for continued growth in Q3, including mass production of CPO switches.

x_themes.topic: Micron's Hiroshima investment and memory market price hikes
x_themes.summary: Micron is investing 1.5T Yen in Hiroshima, while the broader memory market anticipates NAND shortages into 2027 and significant price increases for DDR contracts and HBM orders in 2026-2027.

x_themes.topic: Agility Robotics' commercialization and NVIDIA Halos integration
x_themes.summary: Agility Robotics is advancing its Digit humanoids with significant funding and deployments, leading in safety by being the first to adopt NVIDIA Halos for Robotics, which is critical for mass adoption in industrial workflows.

x_themes.topic: AWS Trainium ASIC shipment forecast increase
x_themes.summary: Amazon AWS has raised its Q3 ASIC server shipment forecast by 20-30% for Trainium3 chips, signaling strong momentum and potentially accelerating the launch of Trainium4.

x_themes.topic: JD.com and Amazon's robot adoption and job displacement
x_themes.summary: JD.com's CEO warns robots will replace 700,000 delivery workers, with plans to retrain them for robot maintenance, mirroring Amazon's strategy to avoid future hires with robotics.

x_themes.topic: CXMT's bonded DRAM development without EUV lithography
x_themes.summary: China's CXMT is testing a pilot production line for bonded DRAM in Hefei, aiming to achieve high-performance memory without EUV lithography by fabricating and bonding cell arrays and peripheral circuitry on separate wafers.

x_themes.topic: Intel 18A process yield improvement and production ramp
x_themes.summary: Intel has reportedly fixed 18A wafer-to-wafer yield variability, with its Oregon and Arizona facilities now reaching an output of approximately 30,000 wafers per month.

x_themes.topic: POET Technologies' Marvell order cancellation and dilution
x_themes.summary: POET Technologies faces challenges after Marvell cancelled all orders, including initial production units, compounded by a CFO disclosure error and past dilution, impacting its role as an ELS supplier for CPO.

x_themes.topic: AWS increasing liquid cooling for Trainium 3 servers
x_themes.summary: AWS has increased its liquid cooling deployments for Trainium 3 servers from 10% to 30% of new racks, indicating additional revenue upside for suppliers and prompting qualification of new vendors.
There is a shortage of high-power cylindrical cells for Battery Backup Units (BBUs) used in data centers, with Samsung SDI and Panasonic experiencing supply issues due to increased demand.
TSMC is expected to raise its full-year revenue outlook to nearly 40% growth, up from "over-30%", driven by strong orders from clients like Nvidia, AMD, and Apple amid the AI megatrend.

x_themes.topic: BlackBerry QNX embedded software platform adoption
x_themes.summary: BlackBerry's QNX software is widely adopted across automotive, medical devices, and robotics, running on various AI compute silicon from NVIDIA, Qualcomm, and Intel, demonstrating strong growth and high margins.

x_themes.topic: Fireworks AI kernel optimization for GPU throughput
x_themes.summary: Fireworks AI is achieving significantly higher GPU throughput (2.6x to 5.6x) compared to competitors by optimizing low-level GPU code (kernels) with its FireAttention technology, which is crucial amidst HBM scarcity.
